name: MCP Elasticsearch Server
description: Official Elastic MCP server (elastic/mcp-server-elasticsearch) exposing read-oriented tools - list_indices, get_mappings, search, esql, get_shards - for full-text search, ES|QL, and analytics. IMPORTANT: the standalone server is deprecated (v0.4.6, 2025-10-24, critical security updates only); Elastic directs users to the Agent Builder MCP endpoint ({KIBANA_URL}/api/agent_builder/mcp), GA in Elastic 9.2+ and Elasticsearch Serverless, with API key authentication.

evidence.value: Highly accurate full-text search with relevance scoring
Powerful aggregation framework with accurate analytics
Performance degrades with very large indices; requires optimization
Stable cluster operations with automatic shard management
Handles Elasticsearch errors with retry and timeout management
Supports API keys, basic auth, and security features (X-Pack)
Elasticsearch credentials stored locally; accessible to AI
AI can construct arbitrary search and ES|QL queries; blast radius is limited to reads since no write tools exist, but expensive queries and broad data reads remain possible
The official server ships only read-oriented tools (list_indices, get_mappings, search, esql, get_shards); it exposes no document indexing, update, or write tools

index_modification_risk.methodology: Operation authorization testing; raised from 60 because the official Elastic server's tool surface is read-only, unlike earlier community servers with write tools

evidence.value: No index deletion or index management tools are exposed by the official server

index_deletion_risk.methodology: Destructive operation testing; raised from 68 as the official tool surface contains no deletion capability

evidence.value: Comprehensive audit logging available in X-Pack Security
Search results and indexed documents sent to LLM provider
Log data and application logs may contain PII
Field-level security available in X-Pack but requires configuration
Search results shared with LLM provider per their privacy policy
Index names and mappings may reveal data structure
Good documentation but community-maintained with evolving coverage
All queries logged in MCP transaction logs
Official Elastic implementation, open source under Apache 2.0 (Rust, ~686 stars)
Clear but incomplete documentation of supported Elasticsearch APIs
Requires Elasticsearch connection URL and credentials
Performance varies with index size and query complexity (typically 50-500ms)
Built on mature Elasticsearch client libraries

Deliberately narrow read-only surface: search, ES|QL, index listing, mappings, and shard info; no indexing or index management tools. Broader/custom tool coverage now comes from the Agent Builder MCP endpoint

api_coverage.methodology: Feature coverage assessment; lowered from 80 as the official server's surface is narrower than the previously described community coverage

evidence.value: Repository carries a deprecation notice: only critical security updates going forward; users are directed to the Agent Builder MCP endpoint in Elastic 9.2+ / Serverless

community_support.methodology: Community support assessment; lowered from 75 due to the standalone server's deprecation

code-generation.notes: Good for generating search queries and analytics dashboards

customer-support.notes: Excellent for searching support tickets and knowledge bases

content-creation.notes: Useful for content search and recommendation systems

data-analysis.notes: Excellent for log analysis, business intelligence, and data exploration

research-assistant.notes: Ideal for full-text research across large document collections

legal-compliance.notes: Risk of exposing legal documents; requires field-level security

healthcare.notes: High risk of exposing patient data in logs and indices

financial-analysis.notes: Moderate risk; financial transaction data exposure concerns

education.notes: Excellent for teaching search technologies and data analytics

creative-writing.notes: Useful for searching writing archives and research materials

best_for: Data analysts performing log analysis and business intelligence
Researchers conducting full-text search across large document collections
Support engineers searching knowledge bases and troubleshooting tickets
Educators teaching search technology and data analytics
strengths: Highly accurate full-text search with relevance scoring, plus ES|QL query support
Official Elastic implementation, open source under Apache 2.0
Read-only tool surface limits blast radius (no write or index management tools)
Excellent for log analysis and business intelligence
Clear migration path to the GA Agent Builder MCP endpoint with custom tools
limitations: DEPRECATED: standalone server receives only critical security updates; Elastic directs users to the Agent Builder MCP endpoint (Elastic 9.2+/Serverless)
Search results and indexed documents exposed to LLM provider
Log data may contain PII and sensitive information
Elasticsearch credentials/API keys stored in local client configuration
Arbitrary search and ES|QL queries can be expensive on large indices
Field-level security requires platform security features and careful configuration

supported_platforms: All platforms (Rust binary, Docker); Agent Builder MCP via Kibana endpoint
metadata.status: Deprecated (critical security updates only) as of the v0.4.x line
metadata.successor: Elastic Agent Builder MCP endpoint: {KIBANA_URL}/api/agent_builder/mcp (GA in Elastic 9.2+ and Elasticsearch Serverless)
metadata.authentication: Elasticsearch API keys or username/password; Agent Builder endpoint uses Kibana API keys
